questions.meaning: a narrow surface that continues out from the side of a cliff or wall
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh stood on the narrow ledge of the balcony at his Greams Road home, watching the traffic below and feeling the cool evening breeze.

questions.meaning: a large, wide, open area of land, water, or sky
questions.sample_sentence: When Anirudh visited Marina Beach with Yohann and Suvasana, they were amazed by the vast expanse of the ocean stretching endlessly before them.

questions.meaning: to gather or summon courage, strength, or energy from within
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh had to muster all his courage to perform his mridangam solo in front of the entire audience at the Music Academy concert.

questions.meaning: a long, thin, silver sea fish
questions.sample_sentence: During the family trip to the fish market near the beach, Anirudh's father explained the difference between a herring and a sardine.

questions.meaning: to eat something very fast and eagerly
questions.sample_sentence: After a long cricket practice session, Anirudh and his cousin Vishwak devoured the hot samosas from Sastha Catering Services within minutes.

questions.meaning: a loud, unpleasant, clucking cry
questions.sample_sentence: Nandi the dog started barking loudly at the crow that let out a harsh cackle from the terrace railing.

questions.meaning: a sea fish with flesh that contains a lot of natural oil
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh's mother prepared a delicious mackerel fry for dinner that the whole family enjoyed, especially Prahlad who was visiting from Bangalore.

questions.meaning: in a delicate and graceful manner
questions.sample_sentence: Suvasana daintily arranged the rangoli patterns outside the classroom door during the Pongal celebrations at Sishya school.

questions.meaning: a very steep, high cliff face
questions.sample_sentence: During the school trip to Kodaikanal, Anirudh peered over the edge of the precipice and felt his heart race at the enormous drop below.

questions.meaning: cleaning and arranging feathers with the beak or tongue
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh watched a peacock preening its brilliant feathers in the garden near Tidel Park, carefully straightening each one with its beak.

questions.meaning: to sharpen or to stimulate (appetite or interest)
questions.sample_sentence: The aroma of freshly made filter coffee from the kitchen was enough to whet Anirudh's appetite even before breakfast was served.

questions.meaning: in an unkind or rude, teasing manner
questions.sample_sentence: Yohann laughed mockingly when Anirudh dropped an easy catch during the cricket match, but later apologised and said he was only joking.

questions.meaning: in a sad, sorrowful manner
questions.sample_sentence: Nandi whined plaintively at the front door when Anirudh left for school each morning, looking up with his big, sad eyes.

questions.meaning: side by side, facing or moving in the same direction
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh and Prahlad walked abreast along the Marina Beach promenade, chatting about the upcoming CSK cricket season.

questions.meaning: began; started
questions.sample_sentence: The annual sports day at Sishya commenced with a grand march-past of all four houses, and Anirudh proudly carried his house flag.

questions.meaning: long narrow raised lines along the surface of something
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh ran his fingers along the ridges of the mridangam's leather surface, feeling the texture before starting his Dhrupad practice session.

questions.meaning: provoked or mocked with insulting remarks
questions.sample_sentence: The opposing team taunted Anirudh's cricket team after winning the first innings, but Anirudh and his teammates came back strongly and won the match.

questions.meaning: acting on natural impulse, without conscious thought
questions.sample_sentence: When the cricket ball came flying towards his face during practice, Anirudh instinctively ducked and the ball flew over his head.

questions.meaning: scolded or rebuked severely
questions.sample_sentence: The teacher upbraided the students for not completing their homework on time, especially since the assignment had been given a whole week earlier.

questions.meaning: the act of becoming fully aware of something
questions.sample_sentence: The realisation that his Dhrupad music exam was only three days away made Anirudh practice twice as hard with Patri Satish Kumar sir.

questions.meaning: in a mocking, scornful, contemptuous manner
questions.sample_sentence: The senior students looked derisively at the juniors struggling with the obstacle course during sports day, forgetting that they had once been beginners too.

questions.meaning: lack of bravery; being afraid to face danger
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh knew that refusing to bat first in the cricket final would be seen as cowardice, so he volunteered to open the innings bravely.

questions.meaning: extremely hungry
questions.sample_sentence: After swimming for two hours at the club, Anirudh was so famished that he ordered three dosas and a plate of idlis at the canteen.

questions.meaning: flying or rising high in the air
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh watched the kites soaring high above Marina Beach during the Pongal festival, their colourful tails streaming behind them in the wind.

questions.meaning: a sudden jump or dive into something
questions.sample_sentence: Anirudh took the plunge and signed up for the inter-school debate competition, even though public speaking made him nervous at first.
